title: Air Fryer Baked Oats
ingredientLines: Ẅ cup old-fashion rolled oats (gluten-free if needed)
Ẅ large over-ripe banana (more brown spots = sweeter)
3 tbsp almond milk or oat milk (I love vanilla unsweetened almond milk)
Optional Sweetness: 1 tbsp honey or maple syrup
Flavor Ideas: 1 tbsp peanut butter or 1 tbsp almond butter
Mix-in Ideas: 2 tbsp chocolate chips,2 Tbsp blueberries, extra banana slices on top, 2 tbs rainbow sprinkles, 2 tablespoons unsweetened shredded coconut
directionLines: 1. Preheat your air fryer to 290ḞF and grease a single-serve ramekin or baking dish.

2. Add all the base ingredients to a high-speed blender (do not add mix-ins yet). Decide if you want to add nut butter (this is optional, but I love peanut butter). Blend on high for 20 â 30 seconds until fully combined.
3. Stir in your desired mix-in. I love chocolate chips, blueberries, or sprinkles.
4. Pour the batter into a greased ramekin (I used an 8-ounce one). Top with the extra chocolate chips or your desired mix-in.
5. Air fry at 290ḞF for 12 - 15 min total. Check at the 7-minute mark since all air fryers are different. If it is browning too quickly on top, feel free to add a piece of aluminum foil on top.
6. The oats should be cooked through and slightly browned on top - enjoy!
